checks.detail: artifacts/system_dependency_priority_report.json (top-level) and artifacts/tls/system_dependency_priority_report.json (TLS sibling). build_tls_dependency_priority.py --top-level-out publishes both atomically from one rank result, so they should be identical immediately after a build. Drift is possible if only one path is regenerated.

checks.check: top-level artifact diverges from TLS artifact
checks.detail: Both bytes match on disk after the latest build (same size, same content).
build_dashboard_3ls_with_tls.py runs the TLS build script before next build and fails closed if required artifacts are missing.

checks.check: dashboard displays stale artifacts as valid
checks.detail: Threshold is 14 days; loader falls back to file mtime when generated_at is missing. Ranking module never emits generated_at, so a recompute can produce a 'fresh' artifact whose loader-derived timestamp is the publish mtime regardless of repository state. Top 3 will render as actionable even when the underlying ranking is stale.

checks.check: recompute regenerates only graph but not priority
checks.detail: Current recompute does call the priority script first, then the optional graph validation script. However, it does NOT explicitly invoke build_dashboard_3ls_with_tls.py, so dashboard integration artifacts may go un-refreshed.

checks.check: dashboard shows registry labels not in registry
checks.detail: registryContract.ts rejects unknown / future / demoted graph nodes. However the recompute candidate list (HOP,MET,METS) and the loader recompute_command default both reference MET and METS, which are NOT registry-active. The candidates flag is documented as advisory-only ('the governed priority artifact still ranks the full registry'), so this does not invent graph nodes — it only confuses the operator.

fix_recommendations_before_phase_1: Emit generated_at from ranking.py (UTC ISO-8601) so the freshness gate has a real source of truth rather than file mtime.
Update PRIORITY_RECOMPUTE_COMMAND to use registry-backed candidates (HOP,RAX,RSM,CAP,SEC,EVL,OBS,SLO).
Reduce the priority artifact stale threshold default to 24h and make it configurable via D3L_PRIORITY_STALE_HOURS.
Expand recompute pipeline to invoke build_dashboard_3ls_with_tls.py --skip-next-build so all dashboard-critical artifacts refresh in one click.
outcome: ack — phase 1 will implement these fixes.
